alerts.title: Ceasefire between US and Iran deteriorates as both sides prepare new strikes
alerts.summary: After initial 28-29 June stand-down agreement, military indicators show continued strike readiness. Multiple CENTCOM reports confirm US conducted additional Iran strikes on 30 June; Iranian military maintains active posture amid Strait of Hormuz tanker attacks. Diplomatic framework fractured—Iran's FM Araghchi hardened demands in Baghdad talks, insisting full Iranian control of Hormuz and exclusion of foreign forces. Breakout risk window remains 1-2 weeks. Sources: incidents (CENTCOM, BBC, CBS News, AP News), telegram (Rybar ~355K views).
alerts.region: Persian Gulf, Strait of Hormuz, Iran

alerts.title: Israel intensifies multi-front operations in Gaza, Lebanon, and southern Syria simultaneously
alerts.summary: IDF confirmed strikes in southern Syria (34.98°N, 38.12°E) forcing civilian evacuation; concurrent Israeli strikes killed 3+ Palestinians including children in Gaza central zone; Lebanon state media reports southern border strikes. Hezbollah condemns new ceasefire arrangement. Marks coordinated escalation across three territorial sectors despite US-Iran pause. Sources: incidents (Al Jazeera, BBC, Reuters, Times of Israel).

alerts.title: US maintains three carrier strike groups and submarine presence in high-readiness posture
alerts.summary: USS Dwight D. Eisenhower (Carrier, Red Sea 15.09°N, 42.40°E), USS Abraham Lincoln (Carrier, Arabian Sea 21.03°N, 59.65°E), and USS Gerald R. Ford (Carrier, Mediterranean 33.98°N, 30.26°E) deployed. USS Florida (Submarine, Persian Gulf 26.13°N, 52.42°E) positioned for contingency response. Three destroyers (Carney, Mason, Laboon) and amphibious assets (Bataan, Boxer) maintain forward presence. Asset concentration indicates sustained deterrence against Iranian escalation. Sources: assets feed (live-osint confirmed positioning).
alerts.region: Red Sea, Arabian Sea, Persian Gulf, Mediterranean

alerts.title: Rybar reports Iran's FM demands restructured Persian Gulf security architecture excluding US
alerts.summary: Rybar broadcast (~355K views, 28 June) covering FM Abbas Araghchi's Baghdad visit detailed Iranian hardline position: Hormuz Strait return to full Iranian control, Persian Gulf security framework rewritten without foreign interference. Signals diplomatic collapse trajectory parallel to kinetic cycle. Indicates Iran weaponizing negotiations amid active military posture. Sources: telegram (Rybar).

alerts.title: IAEA inspector access remains contingent on incomplete final deal; breakout window static at 1-2 weeks
alerts.summary: Iran maintains 60% U-235 enrichment. IAEA confirms inspector access tied to final agreement completion—condition Tehran rejects. Multiple nuclear headlines (IAEA chief, Foundation for Defense of Democracies) underscore inspection dispute escalation. No advancement on breakout timeline reduction. Global threat level static at CRITICAL (9/10). Sources: global_metrics (IAEA headlines, nuclear status tracking).

analysis_note: US-Iran ceasefire framework fractured within 48 hours; Iran hardened negotiating position on Hormuz/Gulf security while maintaining strike-ready posture and 60% enrichment; Israel escalated multi-theater operations (Gaza/Lebanon/Syria); diplomatic pathway narrowing while kinetic risk remains elevated at CRITICAL threat level.
